sglang.title: Use ipc instead of tcp in zmq
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: IPC should be slightly faster than TCP

sglang.title: Make input_ids a torch.Tensor
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: input_ids in ScheduleBatch can be a torch.Tensor instead of a python list. This is more efficient.

Mini fix in sample input to CoT-reasoning.
Modifications

sglang.title: [Fix] Fix major performance bug in certain cases
sglang.author: Ying1123
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
The issue could happen in non-streaming scenarios with small batch sizes. It could break continuous batching because of the wrong manipulation of do_not_get_new_batch.
This PR fixes the issue.

vllm.title: [Misc] Move registry to its own file
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: To avoid polluting the vllm.model_executor.models namespace, I have moved the model registry and its related code into its own registry.py file. ModelRegistry itself remains accessible in vllm.model_executor.models. For convenience, I have also made the classes and functions in vllm.model_executor.m

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Reshape the dimensions of the input image embeddings in Qwen2VL
vllm.author: whyiug
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Reshape the dimensions of the input image embeddings to match those of pixel_values.
Without this adjustment, a dimension mismatch error will occur during batch inference when processing image embeddings.
Related to this previous issue #8856
    image_embeds = ……
    # mm_data['image'] = image_embed

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Flash attention arches not getting set properly
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt: After #8845 the flash attention arches were not getting set correctly since in the flash attention sub-repo the CMakeList.txt depends on VLLM_GPU_ARCHES (not something readily obvious). The #8845 PR stopped uses VLLM_GPU_ARCHES for CUDA in favor of setting the gencodes PR file. This is a quick fix (

vllm.title: [Model] add a bunch of supported lora modules for mixtral
vllm.author: prashantgupta24
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
all-linear option for mixtral produces these target modules:
"target_modules": [
    "o_proj",
    "k_proj",
    "v_proj",
    "w2",
    "w1",
    "gate",
    "q_proj",
    "w3"
  ],

This PR adds the missing modules (w1, w2, w3 and gate) to the supported_lora_modules in mixtral.
Updated mixtral LoR

vllm.title: [Kernel] Zero point support in fused MarlinMoE kernel + AWQ Fused MoE
vllm.author: ElizaWszola
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:

This PR adds zero point support to MarlinMoE kernel in order to enable running it on AWQ.
This PR also adds AWQ Fused MoE support which uses the Marlin MoE Kernel
This PR also cleans up how we pass arguments to fused_marlin_moe().

Testing:

Tested using unit tests in tests/kernels/test_aw

vllm.title: [Misc] Improved prefix cache example
vllm.author: Imss27
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Too few prompts can not show the power of prefix cache. Thus, previous example shows big variance between different runs. By increasing the number of prompts, it produces a much more stable speedup result while not significantly increasing the runtime as a simple example.
FIX #8890
BEFORE SUBMITTING

vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
one step of #8821
the custom allreduce is annoying to torch.compile:

it has runtime size check
and it has either in-place mutation or out-of-place computation

we want to hide this into custom op, but it is not supported in pytorch as of 2.4 .
because the shape check is inside dynamo graph capture,

vllm.title: [Bugfix] use blockmanagerv1 for encoder-decoder
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The default block manager v2 cannot support mllama multi-modal model with text-only request. (can be reproduced by pytest -vs test_mllama.py::test_models). The bug is related to encoder-decoder support of block manager v2. So fallback to v1 for all encoder-decoder models.
